West Asia Conflict Begins Impacting Exports from Telugu States

The ongoing West Asia conflict is starting to impact exports from Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, particularly in agriculture and MSME sectors.

Key Developments:

• Fruit Exports Hit: Export orders for banana and mango from Gulf countries have been cancelled, forcing excess stock into domestic markets.

• Egg Price Pressure: Egg prices have fallen to ₹3.30–₹3.50 per egg, while production cost is ~₹4.50, leading to losses for poultry farmers.

• Seafood & Rice Impact: Rice and shrimp exports are slowing due to shipping disruptions and logistics delays.

• MSME Export Challenges: Pharma, engineering and other MSME exporters are facing delays in shipments and higher freight costs.

Implication:  
Prolonged disruptions could pressure agri exporters, seafood exporters and MSME exporters in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana if the conflict continues.

reports.text:
NIFTY Insight – 25 Years of Monthly Data

Over the last 25 years, analysis of monthly NIFTY 50 data shows an interesting pattern between past returns and future performance.

• Only 17% of months saw past returns fall below 5%.

• In those cases, 77% of the time NIFTY delivered >13% returns over the next 1 year.  
  (≈ 13% is the long-term average annual return of NIFTY based on historical data.)

• Across all months, NIFTY has delivered more than 13% return after 1 year in about 48% of observations.

• Current Situation: NIFTY past return stands at ~6%, close to the lower-return zone historically associated with stronger forward returns.

Observation:  
Many of the periods when past returns were low were also times when pessimism about India or the global economy was very high.

Takeaway:  
Historically, lower past returns have often created better forward return probabilities for long-term investors.

Note: Definitions of “past return” and “future return” are provided in the reference image.

Centre Reassesses Timing of 1-GW Offshore Wind Tender

The government is reviewing the timing of a proposed 1-GW offshore wind power tender following consultations with industry stakeholders.

Key Developments:

• Investor Feedback: Developers indicate investor interest may be weaker than expected due to high project costs and input expenses.

• Policy Engagement: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) is consulting with policy institutions and financial organisations to improve investor participation.

• Tender Design Review: Authorities are evaluating whether tender structure and timeline adjustments are needed to attract credible bidders and ensure capacity addition.

• Global Context: Similar offshore wind auctions globally have seen limited participation, reflecting industry-wide challenges.

• Next Steps: A final decision on launching or postponing the tender will be taken after further consultations with industry players and lenders.

• Infrastructure Push: The government is also progressing with Green Energy Corridor Phase III to strengthen renewable energy transmission infrastructure.

Implication: Highlights cost challenges in offshore wind projects, though policy support for renewable infrastructure continues.

Iran Threat Puts Billions in US Tech Data Centres at Risk

Iran’s warning to target US technology infrastructure in the Gulf region has raised concerns about geopolitical risks to global digital infrastructure.

Data Centre Exposure:
• The Gulf region hosts 70+ data centres with ~557–738 MW capacity.  
• These facilities support major global cloud services and enterprise workloads.

Major Tech Presence:
• Key facilities operated by Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft, Google, Oracle and Alibaba Cloud.

Recent Disruption:
• A drone attack on two AWS facilities recently disrupted services for banking apps and stock markets in the UAE.

Cloud Strategy Shift:
• Cloud providers are evaluating redirecting workloads from Gulf data centres to India and Singapore to mitigate geopolitical risks.

Global Business Impact:
• Any disruption could affect ERP systems, supply chains, financial platforms and analytics services used by multinational companies.

Strategic Importance of Gulf:
• The region is a major digital connectivity hub, carrying ~90% of Europe–Asia internet traffic via submarine cables and internet exchange points.

Implication for India:
• Indian companies relying on global cloud platforms for ERP, finance and analytics systems could face indirect disruptions if the situation escalates.

India Lines Up Alternate Power Sources to Meet Summer Demand

The government expects peak electricity demand to reach ~271 GW this summer, up from ~243 GW last year, reflecting strong growth in power consumption.

Gas-Based Power Shortfall:
• Around 8–10 GW shortfall expected from gas-based power plants due to gas supply constraints linked to the West Asia situation.

Coal Capacity Addition:
• India has added ~9 GW of coal-based capacity in the current fiscal year till January.  
• Another ~3 GW expected to be added soon to support rising demand.

Renewable & Other Capacity Additions:
• Wind: +4.6 GW  
• Hydro: +3.3 GW  
• Nuclear: +700 MW

Role of Gas-Based Plants:
• Though a small share of the power mix, gas plants are important for flexible generation during peak demand, especially at night when solar output declines.

Government Strategy:
• Authorities may divert gas from non-priority sectors to power plants and households to manage potential supply disruptions.

Outlook:
• Alternative generation sources and new capacity additions are expected to ensure adequate electricity supply during the peak summer months.

reports.text:
Power Stocks Rally Up to 8% on Surge in Electricity Demand

Shares of power generation and utility companies gained up to 8% amid expectations of strong electricity demand during the upcoming summer season.

Stock Moves:
• Adani Power, JSW Energy, BHEL, Tata Power and Torrent Power rose around 5–8%.  
• NTPC Green Energy surged nearly 15%, touching ₹99.75 during the session.

Sector Performance:
• The BSE Power Index jumped over 3% intraday to ~7,070, though still below its 52-week high of 7,143.

Demand Trend:
• India recorded peak power demand of ~224.6 GW at 7 PM on March 10, the highest-ever evening demand.

Power Supply Mix:
• Demand during non-solar hours was supported by wind, hydro, nuclear and coal-based generation.

Outlook:
• Analysts expect higher plant load factors for thermal power plants if summer temperatures remain high.  
• Lower rainfall and snow cover may lead to hydro generation shortfalls, increasing reliance on coal-based power.

Implication: Positive demand outlook for thermal and integrated power companies.

India Retail Inflation (CPI) – February Update

India’s retail inflation (CPI) rose to 3.21% in February, up from 2.74% in January, according to data released by the National Statistical Office (NSO).

Key Drivers:
• Food Inflation: Increased due to sharp price rises in coconut, tomato, cauliflower, and green chillies.  
• Food & Beverages Inflation: 3.35%.  
• Pan, Tobacco & Intoxicants: Inflation recorded at 3.49%.

Core Inflation:  
• Core inflation (excluding food & fuel) remained relatively stable at ~3.4%, indicating moderate underlying price pressures.

Rural vs Urban Inflation:
• Rural Inflation: 3.37%  
• Urban Inflation: 3.02%

State-wise Inflation:
• Highest: Telangana – 5.20%  
• Lowest: Mizoram – ~1.07%

Outlook:  
Economists warn that rising crude oil prices amid West Asia tensions could create upside risks to inflation in the coming months.

West Asia Conflict May Disrupt AC Production in India

Rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia and restrictions on commercial LPG supply are creating challenges for air conditioner (AC) manufacturers.

• LPG Usage: LPG is used in key manufacturing processes such as brazing and powder-coating, and supply constraints could impact production.

• Input Cost Pressure: Shortages and price increases in plastic inputs like polypropylene and polystyrene are adding further pressure.

• Commodity Inflation:  
  – Copper prices up ~25%  
  – Aluminium at record highs  
  Both are increasing overall AC manufacturing costs.

• Production Risk: Industry executives warn that AC production could be affected in the coming days if LPG supply restrictions persist.

• Seasonal Sensitivity: The disruption risk comes just ahead of the peak summer season, when AC demand typically surges.

• Industry Response: Companies are closely monitoring supply chains and adjusting procurement strategies to minimise disruptions.

Implication: Potential cost pressure and supply-side risks for AC manufacturers if the situation continues.

reports.text:
AI Boom Drives $700 Billion Surge in Data Center Lease Commitments  

- Microsoft and Meta Platforms have each committed nearly $50 billion in new data center leases in recent quarters to expand AI infrastructure.  

- These massive deals have pushed total future lease obligations of major cloud firms beyond $700 billion.  

- Other key players — including Amazon and Oracle — are also ramping up investments, fueling fierce competition in cloud and AI computing capacity.  

- Microsoft leads with about $155 billion in future lease commitments, followed by Meta at $104 billion.  

- Most leases cover data centers and server farms, though some include associated office and warehouse facilities.  

- The surge underscores the enormous capital intensity required for AI-driven data processing and cloud infrastructure.  

- Industry leaders caution that server farm and compute capacity shortages are emerging as major constraints amid the AI acceleration wave.  

Key Takeaway: The AI boom is triggering record data center leasing by tech giants, marking a new phase of infrastructure competition and capital expansion.

NSE Appoints 20 Merchant Bankers for Upcoming IPO  

- National Stock Exchange of India (NSE) has appointed 20 merchant bankers and eight law firms to manage its long-awaited initial public offering (IPO).  
- The appointed intermediaries will handle regulatory filings, due diligence, documentation, marketing, and execution of the issue.  
- Key merchant bankers include Morgan Stanley, JP Morgan, Citigroup, HSBC, Kotak Mahindra Capital, HDFC Bank, and Axis Capital.  
- In the unlisted market, NSE’s valuation stands at around ₹4.89 lakh crore, with shares trading near ₹1,975 per share.  
- The exchange recently received regulatory clearance after nearly a decade-long delay linked to the co-location case.  
- Last year, NSE made a ₹1,300 crore provision to settle related regulatory matters.  

Key Takeaway: With regulatory hurdles largely behind, NSE’s IPO — one of India’s most awaited listings — is finally moving closer to market debut.

MSMEs Likely to Increase Use of TReDS Financing Amid Trade Uncertainty  

- Rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia are expected to drive higher working capital demand through the Trade Receivables Discounting System (TReDS).  
- Export-oriented MSMEs are facing shipment delays, higher freight costs, and rising insurance premiums, tightening liquidity.  
- In volatile trade conditions, firms prefer regulated, bank-backed financing platforms like TReDS for steady cash flow and supplier continuity.  
- Cross-border invoice flows on receivables platforms are already moderating due to Gulf-region trade disruptions.  
- The UAE, particularly Jebel Ali Port, remains a vital logistics hub — with 10% of India’s merchandise exports heading there.  
- MSMEs contribute about 45% of India’s exports, underscoring their exposure to ongoing shipping bottlenecks.  
- Companies are increasingly turning to invoice factoring and credit lines to navigate payment delays and rising costs.  
- If disruptions persist, analysts expect greater reliance on TReDS for quick financing and improved working capital management.  

Key Takeaway: Trade route volatility is set to accelerate MSMEs’ adoption of TReDS platforms as a safer, faster liquidity lifeline during global supply chain stress.

Key Takeaway: Honda is tightening its EV playbook — prioritising affordability, localisation, and hybrid strength to stay competitive in the evolving Indian EV landscape.

> 🚀 Skyroot Eyes Space-Based Data Centres as Vikram‑1 Launch Nears  
> *India’s leading private space player targets next-gen computing infrastructure in orbit.*

- Skyroot Aerospace is exploring space-based data centres to handle AI and HPC workloads, leveraging lower latency and reduced cooling costs in orbit.  
- The company’s Vikram‑1 rocket launch is nearing completion after earlier technical delays.  
- Plans are underway to scale manufacturing capacity — shifting from one rocket per month to multiple launches per day over time.  
- Co-founder Pawan Kumar Chandana outlined a broader vision of developing rockets for future space infrastructure, including orbital computing capabilities.  
- Skyroot is awaiting IN-SPACe clearances for upcoming missions.  
- Globally, space data processing and storage are emerging as new growth frontiers for launch providers and AI infrastructure players alike.  

Key Takeaway: Skyroot is evolving beyond launch services — positioning itself at the intersection of space tech and data infrastructure, a potential game-changer in India’s private space ecosystem.

🚨 Gas Supply Curbs Threaten Auto Production 
 
Tight LPG, PNG & Propane supplies begin to hit India’s automobile manufacturing chain.

- Tightening fuel availability is disrupting auto component production, hitting heat-intensive processes like casting, forging, and paint shops.  

- Government diversion of gas toward household LPG demand has cut supply to industries.  

- SIAM has urged authorities to restore gas allocations to auto OEMs and suppliers.  

- MSME component makers face operational strain; few have quick-switch options to alternative fuels.  

- ACMA warns that foundries and forging units are already affected, risking further supply disruptions.  

- Production delays may emerge if fuel shortages persist, impacting overall vehicle output and inventory levels.  

- Stable gaseous fuel supply remains critical for seamless auto manufacturing cycles.

Key Takeaway: Prolonged gas curbs could slow component output, delay vehicle deliveries, and hit the auto sector’s near-term momentum.

STOCK MARKET WEALTH MYTH EXPOSED

Bessembinder's Shocking Study  
- 58% of 26,000+ stocks underperformed Treasury Bills  
- ~75% lagged overall market index  
- Just 4% created ALL net wealth in stock market  

Indian Reality  
- Elite compounders like Asian Paints, Titan, HDFC Bank, TCS drive bulk returns  
- Thousands stagnate, underperform, or destroy capital  
- Most "slow growers" still beat market over decades  

Critical Investor Lesson  
Stock markets extremely unequal  
One great business held 10 years > dozens of mediocre stocks  

Key Takeaway:  
Hunt rare compounders—equal-weight portfolios guarantee mediocrity.

reports.text:
IEA Emergency Oil Release May Have Limited Impact

• 🛢️ Strategic Release: IEA plans 400 million barrels emergency oil release to offset Strait of Hormuz supply disruptions.

• ⏳ Short-Term Cover: Release could provide ~32–45 days of supply if deployed quickly.

• ⚙️ Logistics Challenge: Impact depends on timing, delivery speed, and distribution logistics.

• 🏭 Product Mix Issue: Stockpile includes both crude and refined products, not all immediately usable by refineries.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Oil release may temporarily stabilize supply, but effectiveness depends on execution and logistics.

🚆 QUADRANT FUTURE TEK: KAVACH SYSTEM ENTERS FINAL FIELD TRIALS

A key milestone in India’s railway safety modernization.

• 🚆 Passenger Field Trials: The company has entered the passenger train field trials stage for the KAVACH (Indian Railways Automatic Train Protection) system.

• 🧪 Highest Safety Compliance: The system has successfully cleared multi layer testing including SIL 4 certification, the highest safety standard for railway signalling systems.

• 📡 Next Key Trigger: After successful completion of field trials, the system is expected to receive RDSO certification, enabling wider deployment across Indian Railways.

• 📦 Strong Order Book: As of February 2026, the company has an executable order book of around ₹898 Cr.

• 🛠️ Large Execution Scope: Current contracts include KAVACH installation in 768 locomotives and coverage of 1,109 RKM of railway tracks.

🟢 Impact: Positive

Reaching the final trial stage significantly strengthens the visibility of commercial rollout for railway safety systems, positioning the company well for future railway modernization projects. 📈

reports.text: Mirae Asset Sharekhan Morning Tiger ( Pre - Market Insight ) - 12th March 2026.pdf

Why India Can't Ditch LPG for Electric Stoves Yet

Power Reality Check  
1.7 GW Data Centers (10 GW by 2030) could power 1 Cr kitchens — but locked 24/7 vs homes' 2-3 hr daily use.

Surplus Myth  
520 GW installed vs 250 GW peak demand — but grid can't deliver.

Root Cause: Last-Mile Crisis  
- Thin distribution lines/transformers melt under peak cooking load (200 GW if 10 Cr homes switch at dinner).  
- DISCOMs bankrupt (₹7L Cr debt), theft/subsidies/corruption kill efficiency.  
- "One Bulb" scarcity design since Independence.  

Peak Hour Nightmare  
10 Cr families x 2 KW = grid collapse 7-9 PM nationwide.

China vs India  
China: Infra ahead of demand — UHV lines, 99.9% reliability, LPG industrial-only.  
India: Demand-first, subsidy-driven — thin lines, perpetual shortages.

Key Takeaway  
India's energy poverty stems from last-mile grid failure despite surplus capacity — LPG stays essential until trillion-rupee upgrades or solar/smart grids arrive.

**LNG vs LPG Shortage Impact: Industry Breakdown**

**Core Differences**  
**LNG** (Liquefied Natural Gas): Methane-based, cryogenic (-162°C), used for power generation, industry, city gas.  
**LPG** (Liquefied Petroleum Gas): Refinery byproduct (propane/butane), cylinder-based, used for cooking, heating, autos.

**LNG Consumption Split (India)**  
- **Power**: ~45% (45 MMSCMD) – Gas plants face load cuts.  
- **Fertilizer**: ~25% (25 MMSCMD) – Urea production hit.  
- **City Gas (CGD)**: ~15% (15 MMSCMD) – CNG autos, PNG homes.  
- **Refineries/Petrochem**: ~10% (10 MMSCMD) – Feedstock squeeze.  
- **Others (Steel/Cement)**: ~5% (5 MMSCMD) – Industrial kilns.

**Industry Impacts**  
**Power**: Forced coal shift (+ve Coal India/NTPC); spot tariffs spike.  
**Fertilizer**: Subsidy burden up; urea imports surge.  
**CGD (IGL/MGL)**: CNG price hikes; PNG residential cutbacks.  
**Ceramics/Glass**: Kiln shutdowns (Somany hit earlier).  
**Hotels/Catering**: LPG pivot strained (just reported shortages).

**LPG Shortage Preview**  
Refinery cuts → cylinder queues → rural distress → organized players gain.

**Key Takeaway**  
LNG crunch hits power/fertilizer hardest (70% usage); LPG pinch squeezes hospitality/rural — coal/power stocks resilient amid energy pivot.

Right Issue Arbitrage: Real Opportunity or Trap?

Issue Snapshot  
Right issue at ₹10/share (vs CMP ₹115) in 1:1 ratio — headline arbitrage of ₹105/share (less fees/taxes).

Arbitrage Reality  
Yes, it's real and executable for eligible shareholders:  
- Apply at ₹10, get shares allotted.  
- Sell in open market at CMP for instant ₹105 gross gain/share.  
- Caveats: Renounce rights (if allowed) for premium; STT/CGT ~20-25% tax hit; liquidity risk if oversubscribed/delayed allotment.

Kurlon Stake Status  
- Company previously held significant stake in Kurlon Enterprises (mattress firm).  
- Sheela Foam acquired ~94.7% majority in 2023 (~₹2,035 Cr deal); recent buyback for remaining 5.34% at ₹588/share.  
- Current holding: Likely negligible/zero post-acquisition & buyback — Sheela Foam now controls Kurlon.

Key Takeaway  
₹105 arbitrage is genuine for rights holders (post-tax ~₹80 net), but verify company's balance sheet dilution risk — Kurlon stake now minimal.

Market Flash: Iran Oil Shock Deepens, Key Updates

Iran Crisis & Oil Chaos  
- IEA slashes 2026 demand growth to 640k bpd (from 850k); 8 Mn bpd March supply plunge — history's largest disruption.

reports.text:
Chinese Brands Pivot to Online Sales Amid Cost Pressures

Strategic Channel Shift  
- OnePlus, Realme and other Chinese smartphone brands ramp up online sales focus to combat rising costs and stay competitive.  
- Triggered by memory/storage prices tripling YoY, fueled by AI data centre demand.  

Cost-Cutting Measures  
- Reducing/restructuring offline sales teams and slashing store-related expenses.  
- Offline retail costs 20–24% of product price vs online's 8–10%, making e-commerce far more economical.  
- Outsourcing marketing and in-store promoters to third-party agencies to trim fixed costs.  

Market Risks  
- Heavy online reliance risks straining ties with traditional retailers and distributors.  
- Brands already hiking prices on sub-₹40,000 smartphones to offset component inflation.  

Supply Outlook  
- Industry estimates memory shortages persisting until 2027, keeping component prices elevated.  

Key Takeaway:  
Chinese smartphone majors' online pivot delivers cost efficiencies but tests offline partnerships amid prolonged memory supply constraints.

SME Stocks Wealth Destruction: Key Lessons

Core Investing Principle  
- Extraordinary returns aren't essential – capital protection is paramount.  
- Wealth Protection First, Returns Later: Safe capital + patience = inevitable growth.  

Golden Rule  
- Never risk core capital chasing quick gains in speculative SME plays.  
- High-risk bets belong in satellite portfolios, not life savings.  

Practical Wisdom  
- Protect Capital → Stay Patient → Wealth Will Follow.  
- Time rewards disciplined investors; greed destroys impatient ones.  

Key Takeaway:  
SME wealth destruction teaches that surviving market cycles with intact capital guarantees long-term success over chasing lottery-ticket returns.

Promoter Buying Signals Confidence

Time Technoplast  
- Promoters actively increasing stake through open market purchases.  
- Recent buying shows strong management conviction in growth prospects.  

Aarti Pharmalabs  
- Promoter stake accumulation signals faith in pharma business trajectory.  
- Insider buying typically precedes positive operational developments.  

Apollo Pipes  
- Management backing company with personal capital deployment.  
- Promoter purchases highlight undervaluation perception at current levels.  

Investor Signal

Key Takeaway:

Fuel Crisis Cripples Gig Drivers Amid Geopolitical Tensions

Supply Disruptions Hit Hard  
- Ola, Uber, and Rapido drivers face LPG and CNG shortages due to geopolitical tensions impacting fuel supply chains.  
- Long queues at fuel stations with 2–2.5 hour waits and lines stretching up to 1 km in major cities.  

Cost Surge in Key Markets  
- In Hyderabad, LPG prices for auto drivers jumped from ₹65.70/kg to ₹92.97/kg, sharply raising operating costs.  
- Fewer refills mean fewer rides completed daily, directly slashing driver earnings.  

Company Response  
- Ride-hailing platforms are monitoring closely and engaging government authorities to reduce disruptions for drivers and customers.  

Key Takeaway:  
Fuel shortages and price hikes threaten gig economy stability, forcing drivers into survival mode with eroding incomes and reduced ride supply.

**Sector Output Impacts**  
- **Fertilisers, petrochemicals**: Production disruptions expected.  
- **Restaurants, glass, ceramics**: Significant capacity hits.

- **OMCs**: IOC, BPCL, HPCL face **margin compression**.

LPG/LNG supply crunch threatens key industries short-term — OMCs/gas utilities vulnerable, but petrol/diesel exports provide defensive buffer.

US & Inflation Update  
- US markets ended mixed — Dow Jones down 289 pts, while Nasdaq edged higher led by energy, tech, and communication stocks.  
- US CPI rose 2.4% YoY in Feb; monthly inflation at 0.3%, core inflation at 2.5% — keeping Fed rate-cut hopes in check.  

Geopolitical Tensions Intensify  
- Shipping risks escalate near Strait of Hormuz as tanker traffic slows and insurers step in.  
- Oil surged over 4% — WTI up nearly 5% — amid fears of supply disruption from the US–Iran conflict.  
- Iran warns crude could hit $200/barrel if its exports are curtailed.  
- IEA to release about 400 million barrels from reserves; US DOE to free 172 million barrels from SPR.  
- Brent crude now at $98.3/barrel; gold near $5,159 and silver around $85.6.  

Domestic Market Recap  
- Nifty slid 1.6%; Jio Financial Services was top gainer, Bajaj Finance biggest loser.  
- Nifty Bank lost 2.1%, breaking below 56,000 with Axis Bank leading declines.  
- Midcap and Smallcap indices also ended lower; India VIX jumped 11.4% signaling heightened volatility.  
- Nifty Pharma rose for a 2nd session led by IPCA Labs and Glenmark Pharma.  
- Nifty Energy gained with Adani Total Gas hitting upper circuit.  
- Nifty Auto fell 3.2%, TVS Motor worst performer.  
- Nifty IT down 1.2%, led by Coforge and TCS.  
- Nifty FMCG slipped 1.1%; Colgate-Palmolive (India) plunged nearly 6.5%.  
- Nifty Metal declined with APL Apollo Tubes and JSW Steel under pressure.  

Key Takeaway:  
Rising oil prices and Middle East tensions are driving safe-haven buying in gold but weighing on Indian markets, signaling continued volatility ahead.

**Fresh Coverage Initiation**  
- **Piramal Finance** – *Buy*, Target **₹2,150**  
- **L&T Finance** – *Buy*, Target **₹325**  
- **Tata Capital** – *Buy*, Target **₹400**  
- **HDB Finance** – *Neutral*, Target **₹760**  

**Growth Outlook**  
- Nomura expects all four NBFCs to post **high-teens to 20%+ loan growth CAGR** over **FY26–28**.  
- **Three of the four** to see **ROE expansion beyond mid-teens** by FY28 as profitability strengthens.  
- Sector theme: **"Diversified lenders – Everyone, everywhere, all the time."**  

**Stock-Specific Views**  
- **Tata Capital** – Strong loan growth trajectory with ample headroom for **profitability expansion**.  
- **Piramal Finance & L&T Finance** – Undergoing **structural transformations**; both seen as **long-term compounders** with meaningful **upside potential**.  
- **HDB Finance** – Needs **rebound in loan growth** momentum for a **re-rating catalyst**.  

**Sector Insight**  
- Nomura highlights Indian regulators’ **preparation for AI adoption** across lending ecosystems, supporting digital scalability and prudent risk management.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Nomura sees India’s leading diversified NBFCs entering a new growth cycle – led by digital transformation, improving ROEs, and strong balance-sheet discipline.

GAS SHORTAGE DISRUPTS INDUSTRY & CONSUMPTION PATTERNS  

Supply Crunch Widens  
- MGL cuts gas supply to industrial and commercial customers.  
- GAIL (India) LNG allocation from Petronet LNG reduced to zero (effective Mar 4).  
- GNFC faces curbs — GAIL restricted LNG allocation to 60% (from Mar 6), impacting neem-coated urea output.  
- BPCL’s halt in LPG supply triggers cascading disruptions across sectors.  

Manufacturing Disruptions Emerge  
- Hindustan Organic Chemicals temporarily shuts its Cumene plant due to halted LPG supply.  
- Borosil suspends production at its borosilicate glass furnace; opal glass furnaces running at lower capacity.  
- EPACK Durable halts production meant for the Middle East market.  

Hospitality Sector Feels The Heat  
- Royal Orchid Hotels: LPG supply to commercial users stopped; most hotels facing operational challenges.  
- Kamat Hotels: Switching to electric kitchens costs 40–45% more than LPG-based cooking.  

Consumer Shift Underway  
- Amazon India: Induction cooktop sales up 30x in two days; pressure cookers and rice cookers up 4x.  
- Flipkart: Induction cooktop sales quadrupled over last 4–5 days versus prior weeks; now seen as a “survival necessity.”  
- Stovekraft: Demand for electric cooktops surged 3x last week.  

Analyst View  
- Elara Capital’s Karan Taurani: Quick Service Restaurants (QSRs) may see a shift in demand as customers pivot toward chains using electric-based cooking setups.  

Key Takeaway:  
Widespread LPG and gas supply disruptions are crippling key industries while accelerating India’s short-term transition toward electric-based cooking and manufacturing solutions.

**UBS on Bajaj Auto: Maharashtra 3W Permit Freeze**

**Policy Impact**  
- Maharashtra govt **halts new auto-rickshaw permits** to curb congestion/pollution.  
- Targets **traffic management** in key urban centers.

**Bajaj Exposure**  
- **~87% market share** in Maharashtra **ICE 3W** segment.  
- State contributes **17%** of domestic ICE 3W sales (ex-Telangana).

**Margin Vulnerability**  
- **ICE 3Ws** generate **>30% margins**—high sensitivity to volume slowdown.  
- Policy directly crimps **new registrations**.

**Risk Assessment**  
- **Negative near-term** on volumes/margins.  
- EV transition may mitigate long-term, but ICE dominance hurts short.

**Key Takeaway**  
**Permit freeze** pressures Bajaj's high-margin 3W engine—watch for EV pivot acceleration.

Portfolio additions & deletions by Nemish Shah, Ashish Kacholia, Mukul Agrawal, Anil Kumar Goel, Rakesh Jhunjhunwala, Ashish Dhawan, Hemendra Kothari, Vijay Kedia, Madhusudan Kela, Akash Bhanshali, etc as of December 2025 quarter

Every time since 2001 that the benchmark index has stayed flat for ~17 months, a strong rally has followed.

History shows ~30% average gains in the next 12 months & ~76% over the following 3 yrs.

This is the 14th such instance — and investors are watching closely. 

C: ETMarket

IndiaMART is suing OpenAI.

IndiaMART discovered last October that ChatGPT has been completely ignoring it while competitor platforms like TradeIndia continue to appear normally. 

The US Trade Representative (USTR) has flagged IndiaMART as a "notorious market" for counterfeits and OpenAI, wary of liability, simply stopped surfacing it.

IndiaMART filed a petition in the Calcutta High Court on 27 December, arguing selective discrimination. OpenAI's defense is that it has no office or permanent establishment in India.

AI is the new search layer and India has no framework recognizing a "right to be surfaced" in an AI response.

GOLDMAN SACHS ON INDIA AUTOS

Believe refineries such as Reliance, IOC, BPCL & HCPL will likely have to partially switch to other sources of fuel such as fuel oil to replace 35% of potential volume curtailment

Nomura GAIL

Expect operational impact on GAIL’s Pata petchem plant which relies completely on gas sourced from Qatar Energy

@Beatthestreetnews

What happens if oil supply from the Persian Gulf is disrupted?

Goldman Sachs models different disruption scenarios:

• 10 day disruption: Brent fair value ~$66
• 21 day disruption: Brent ~$71
• 30 day disruption: Brent $93**

Key insight:
Oil markets react sharply to supply shocks from the Persian Gulf because it accounts for a large share of global crude flows.

Another important dynamic:

• Every $10 increase in Brent can destroy roughly 0.5 to 1.8 mb/d of demand depending on price levels.

Meaning:
Higher oil prices eventually reduce demand, creating a natural stabilizing mechanism for the oil market.

India built strategic buffers for oil. But for gas, we are dangerously exposed.

India holds strategic crude reserves, yet LNG storage covers barely 21 days of inventory, even as gas demand rises and supply routes through West Asia face disruption.

With India importing 85% of its crude and 50% of its gas, any prolonged conflict around Hormuz can quickly tighten LNG supply and spike prices.

Countries like China, Japan and Korea mandate 10% gas storage of annual demand. India has none.

In the next energy shock, the real vulnerability may not be oil. But it could be natural gas.

India may build 8 to 10 GW of data center capacity by 2030 :)

For every 1 GW data center you roughly need 4,000 to 8,000 km cables, about 25,000 to 40,000 tons copper, and the cable cost alone is around ₹3500 to ₹4000 crore per GW.

So if India builds 10 GW, that is roughly a ₹35,000 to ₹40,000 crore cable opportunity.

From this, the Data center segment alone may generate around ₹2500 to ₹3000 crore revenue, and the industry may grow 20 percent plus CAGR

Morgan Stanley on India Energy

Impact of oil shocks in India has varied since price deregulation was introduced in 2014

With oil equities typically underperforming initially but then significantly outperforming in the two years after a shock

See 2026 shaping up for a similar response, and prefer Indian refiners and OMCs

As tends to happen in energy shocks, governments freeze fuel prices

2026 will likely be no different

We think policy stability will be maintained, with no windfall taxes (considering the stability laws passed by parliament in 2025)

Continue to view Indian refiners and OMCs positively and would look to add during underperformance

Indian refiners and gas users are set to play an essential role in reducing supply disruptions in fuel products as they absorb the US natural gas glut in the months ahead

Househelp Apps Cross 2 Million Monthly Orders but Profitability Concerns Grow

India’s instant househelp platforms like Urban Company, Snabbit, and Pronto have crossed 2 million monthly orders in February, up 45–50% from about 1.3 million in December.

Urban Company leads with 840,000–850,000 orders, followed by Snabbit (~830,000) and Pronto (~340,000).

Market share in February:
Urban Company: 49.2%
Snabbit: 36.4%
Pronto: 14.3%

Companies are expanding aggressively into new cities and micro-markets, increasing scale in the 10-minute househelp services segment.

To acquire users, platforms are offering deep discounts, with services priced as low as ₹1 per hour in some markets.

The sector is burning significant cash, with combined spending of about $10–11 million in February.

Investors are increasingly questioning whether the business model can achieve sustainable profitability.

Urban Company’s stock has fallen over 40% since listing, reflecting concerns over unit economics.

**Investor Return Formula: Mental Model of Great Investors**

1. Return = Earnings Growth + P/E Expansion

2. Retail investors miss the 2nd part

3. Infosys (2000), Trent (2024), Eternal (2025): High Starting PE ➝ No PE Expansion ➝ No Return

P/E Re-rating Powers Investor Returns:

Infosys 2000: The Dotcom Bubble

a. In March 2000, Infosys stock traded at a P/E of 200. Investors priced in decades of future growth.

b. 2000 to 2009: Infosys profits grew 20X in 10 yrs (₹286 cr in 2000 to ₹5,819 cr in 2009). Very few companies in the world achieve that growth.

c. Investor Returns from 2000 to 2009: Flat (Zero returns). Why? Because the valuation multiple (P/E) normalized from 200X to 25X (87% decline).

Lessons for Investors

a. P/E multiples normalize over time because growth normalizes over time. New competitors enter, new technologies happen, founders get rich and lazy, consumer preferences change.

b. For example, you may be euphoric today about Eternal’s growth. But over the next 10 years, you may no longer be so euphoric. If their growth normalizes, you could end up with zero returns over 10 years.

c. Retail investors ignore future P/E normalization because that is the hard part to calculate. Legendary investors only focus on that part.

d. P/E re-rating changes everything. If starting P/E is low, and earnings growth improves over time, long-term investor returns can be extraordinary. If the opposite happens, long-term returns will be poor.

When Does a Stock Deserve High P/E?

a. If a company can continue to re-invest its profits at high returns for a long time-period, only then a high P/E multiple is justified. Three conditions must be met:

(1) High return on capital employed (high ROCE)

(2) High re-investment rate (large opportunity to re-invest a major portion of annual profits)

(3) Long duration of annual re-investment cycle (long runway)

b. Example: The following business performance deserves a high P/E multiple:

ROCE = 25%
Re-investment Rate = 60%
Earnings Growth =
ROCE x Reinvestment Rate
25% x 60% = 15% CAGR
Growth Duration: 20 yrs

This is 15% YoY profit growth without any need for external capital (debt or equity dilution). Just 60% of annual profits are re-invested year after year.

c. The critical factor in the above example is how long the re-investment cycle can last? Will it last for 5 years or 20 years? The compounding power of 20 years is extraordinary. But if it lasts only 5 years, and you paid high starting P/E, capital destruction will happen. (Example: Trent)

How Do 100-Bagger Returns Happen: Low Starting P/E, Steady Growth, Long Runway

a. In 2002, Titan’s P/E ratio was 8 or 10 because future growth expectations (which determine P/E multiples) were modest.

b. Titan’s branded jewellery business did not enjoy an explosive growth rate of 25% or 30% YoY. Instead, there was a reasonable growth for a long period. Brand trust increased gradually, store expansion happened steadily.

c. P/E Re-rating: Once the market recognized Titan’s long runway, P/E multiple changed from 10 to 60~80. In other words, investors were willing to pay 8X more for the same stock.

d. MATH: Assume these hypothetical figures for Titan in 2002:

EPS = ₹1
P/E = 10
Stock Price = ₹10

Earnings (EPS) Growth:
15% CAGR x 20 yrs

EPS after 20 yrs = ₹16.37

P/E after 20 yrs = 70

Stock Price = EPS x PE
= ₹16.37 x 70 = ₹1,146

Price Change in 20 Yrs:
₹10 to ₹1,146 = 114X

How did 114X returns happen?

Earnings Growth x PE Expansion

16.37 x 70

Market rewarded shareholders with a massive P/E expansion of 7X because of steady growth, long runway, and low starting P/E.

ENDQUOTE

“In the short-run, the market is a voting machine. (You can vote even for a fool based on narratives.) In the long-run, it is a weighing machine. (Scientific measurement based on unemotional facts.)” – Warren Buffett’s Guru Benjamin Graham, “The Intelligent Investor,” 1949

**BofA India Strategy (Amish Shah): Clear Sector Preferences**

**Defense > EPC Contractors**  
- Strong preference for Defense plays over EPC contractors.  
- Defense offers superior risk-reward amid capex cycle.  

**Preferred Infra Themes**  
- Favor Coal, Wires & Cables, Data centers over Railways/Ports.  
- Avoid Industrials, Infrastructure, EMS, Shipbuilding, Cement.  

**Defensive Picks**  
- Telecom, Hospitals, Pharma preferred over Utilities/Renewables.  
- Staples and Retailers trail in defensive allocation.  

**Energy & Metals**  
- Upstream energy, non-ferrous metals over Downstream energy.  
- Steel remains lower priority versus upstream beneficiaries.  

**Discretionary Winners**  
- QSR, Quick Commerce, Jewelers, Two-wheelers lead the pack.  
- Avoid Airlines/Airports, Hotels, Durables, Real Estate, Beverages.  
- Auto Ancillaries and Tyres also deprioritized.  

**Financials Hierarchy**  
- Exchanges > AMCs & Capital market plays.  
- Banks > NBFCs, Insurance, Housing Finance.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
BofA's Amish Shah constructs clear thematic hierarchy—Defense/Data centers/Upstream lead while Infra/Discretionary laggards trail significantly.
